THE SITUATION IN THE UNITED STATES (Source: "MORBIDITY AND MORTALITY-WEEKLY REPORT", U.S. National Office of Vital Statistics): Number of reported cases for the current week (previous week in parentheses):
Of the 47 cases of diphtheria, 8 were in Texas, 5 in Florida, and 4 each in North Carolina, Alabama, and Mississippi.
For the current week, Minnesota reported 7 cases of typhoid fever. Although the number is small, it is as many as was reported by the state for the entire year of 1955.

The Arizona Commisioner of Health has reported a realtively high incidence of poliomyelitis  during the 4-week period ended January 27, 1956. Of the 15 cases reported, 9 had signs of bulbar involvement, 4 were spinal paralytic, and 2 were non-paralytic. Twelve occurred in one county. There was 1 fatal case in an 8-year old child.
